title: Please publish varlink-enabled podman for Ubuntu
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

Current builds of `podman` don't seem to have `varlink` build tag. This leads to `podman varlink` command being missing. Could you start publishing varlink-enabled `podman` in projectatomic repos?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `apt-get install podman`

2. `podman varlink unix:/run/podman/io.podman`

**Describe the results you received:**
An error message saying:
`Error: unrecognized command 'podman varlink'`


**Describe the results you expected:**
varlink interface to be started up.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
N/A.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.3.0-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.4
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.4
  podman version: 1.3.0-dev
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.8-dev
  Conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/crio/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/crio/conmon
    version: 'conmon version , commit: '
  Distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "18.04"
  MemFree: 7116824576
  MemTotal: 8363900928
  OCIRuntime:
    package: 'cri-o-runc: /usr/bin/runc'
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  hostname: host
  kernel: 4.15.0-47-generic
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 387h 25m 49.85s (Approximately 16.12 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries: []
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 0
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
N/A

comments.body:
This is via the Projectatomic PPA, right?
@lsm5 PTAL

I'd like to bump this one. 
The ubuntu version 1.4.0 from the Projectatomic PPA is still lacking varlib support.
@towe75 @EricHripko I'll get the libvarlink package on the ppa next week. So please hold on a little longer. I added v1.4.3 to the ppa btw.
@lsm5 , @baude  is there a ubuntu specific show-stopper? I'm not a packaging expert but i work mostly on ubuntu machines. Can i test/support anything?
I think our biggest issue is getting libvarlink itself packaged up

Question 1: what's the challenge (did not look at libvarlink, yet)? I'm working a lot with C-ish projects, so maybe i can help something.

Question 2: Is it a necessary dependency? Podman, because of go, will surely not depend on it, same for systemd. I understand that it would be nice to have the varlink cli command for testing purposes, but otherwise i can not see the relationship. Possibly i am wrong, though.
Yeah, https://github.com/varlink/go doesn't seem to use any `cgo`, so it wouldn't use `libvarlink`. That shouldn't be a blocker 🙂

the challenge of not having libvarlink on ubuntu is two fold:

1. when using a remote-client and the bridge technique, connections will fail because libvarlink is **required**.
2. the varlink util to debug connections is not available.
IC. Coming back to libvarlink: why is it a big issue to getting it packed up? I assume it would be published into the very same projectatomic PPA?

> IC. Coming back to libvarlink: why is it a big issue to getting it packed up? I assume it would be published into the very same projectatomic PPA?

1. I'm still setting up autobuild pipelines for ubuntu versions, done with 18.04 and 19.04, in the middle of 16.04 atm.

2. RE: libvarlink deb, I haven't been able to get it built yet, the last few times I tried some days back. I'll revisit that soon as I get the chance, unless you have the deb packaging files ready that I could reuse.
@EricHripko @towe75 varlink enabled packages are just about getting published to ppa for 19.04 and 18.04. PTAL..
closing then ... re-open if a problem arises.
